skill.name: reference-search
skill.description: Multi-database literature search and search-strategy design that outputs structured, reproducible result lists; use when you need reference retrieval, systematic searching, review topic selection, or to construct a traceable search strategy.
skill.summary: Automated PubMed literature search with structured, reproducible results for systematic reviews and evidence retrieval.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 93 static analysis findings are false positives upon evaluation. The skill runs Python scripts to call the PubMed API (NCBIs official E-utilities endpoint only). No hardcoded credentials: the API_KEY config defaults to an empty string and EMAIL is a placeholder. The high-entropy flag on inputs/query.json is caused by Chinese text, not obfuscation. Shell command detections in SKILL.md and evaluation-checklist.md are documentation code examples, not executable code. The audit_result JSON file flagged for weak crypto is a structured JSON evaluation report with no cryptographic operations. The skill is transparent about its network and script execution needs and is safe to publish.

content.user_title: Search PubMed literature with reproducible results
content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours manually searching PubMed and designing search strategies for systematic reviews. This skill automates reference retrieval and search strategy construction to produce structured, reproducible results.
actual_capabilities: Extracts key biomedical terms from title and abstract to build PubMed search queries
Constructs Boolean search queries with field restrictions for PubMed
Searches PubMed via the official NCBI E-utilities API
Parses search results into structured JSON and CSV output files
Supports both interactive text input and JSON file input for automation
Records full query string, keyword list, and result count for reproducibility
limitations: Supports the PubMed database only, not Scopus, Web of Science, or other databases
Does not support complex nested Boolean queries or advanced search operators
Does not deduplicate search results across multiple searches
Requires a valid email address for NCBI API compliance

use_cases.title: Conduct a systematic literature review
use_cases.description: A researcher collects and documents all relevant studies on a clinical question using reproducible PubMed searches.

use_cases.title: Rapid evidence retrieval for clinical questions
use_cases.description: A clinician quickly searches PubMed for the latest evidence on a specific treatment or condition.

use_cases.title: Design and document search strategies
use_cases.description: A librarian or evidence synthesis specialist constructs, tests, and records search strategies for systematic reviews.

prompt_templates.title: Quick PubMed search for a topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Search PubMed for recent literature on the effectiveness of ketamine for treatment-resistant depression. Use the reference-search skill and provide structured results.
prompt_templates.scenario: New user needs a quick literature search

prompt_templates.title: Systematic review search strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: I am conducting a systematic review on probiotics for preventing antibiotic-associated diarrhea in children. Use the reference-search skill to design a search strategy with keywords, synonyms, and Boolean operators. Run the search and export the results.

prompt_templates.title: Custom search with specific scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Search PubMed for studies published in the last 5 years on the association between gut microbiome composition and response to immunotherapy in melanoma patients. Extract keywords from the provided title and abstract, build the query, and export results to CSV.
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced user with specific search parameters

prompt_templates.title: Reproducible multi-step search workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the reference-search skill, perform a structured literature search on CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing for sickle cell disease. First extract keywords from the topic, then construct a PubMed search strategy with field restrictions, execute the search, and provide JSON output with the query string, result count, and full record details. Document the search date and parameters for reproducibility.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user needing fully documented reproducible search

output_examples.input: Search PubMed for literature on cancer cachexia treatments.
output_examples.output: Search completed: 20 results found. The search used the query string: "cancer cachexia"[Title] AND cachexia[Title/Abstract] AND pancreatic[Title/Abstract]. Results include PMID, title, journal, publication date, and authors for each record. Output files: pubmed_search_results.json and pubmed_search_results.csv.

output_examples.input: Find recent literature on immunotherapy for advanced melanoma.
output_examples.output: Search returned 20 records from PubMed. Top results include studies on checkpoint inhibitors and combination therapies published in 2022-2024. All records are available in structured JSON and CSV format for screening.

best_practices: Always provide a complete title and abstract for better keyword extraction and more relevant search results.
Set your institutional email address in the script configuration to comply with NCBI usage policies.
Document the search date, query string, and result count for each search session to maintain reproducibility.
anti_patterns: Do not hard-code email addresses or API keys in the configuration file. Use user prompts or environment variables instead.
Do not modify the script to bypass the rate limiter. Respect NCBI API rate limits to avoid being blocked.
Do not run searches without first confirming the research question, scope, and expected output format with the user.

faq.question: What databases does this skill support?
faq.answer: Currently this skill supports PubMed only through the NCBI E-utilities API.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. An API key is optional and can increase rate limits, but basic functionality works without one.

faq.question: Can I customize the number of results returned?
faq.answer: Yes. Change the RETMAX value in the configuration section of scripts/pubmed_search.py.

faq.question: Does this skill store or share my search data?
faq.answer: No. Search results are saved locally to the outputs directory. The skill does not send data anywhere except to the official PubMed API.

faq.question: How can I make my search reproducible?
faq.answer: Record the search date, query string, keywords, and result count. The skill outputs these in the JSON result file.

faq.question: Can I search multiple databases at once?
faq.answer: No. The current version supports searching PubMed only. For multi-database searches, run separate searches and combine results manually.
